title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 3.1)
context_text: A graph $H$ is called $\chi$-bounding if the ideal of all $H$-free graphs is $\chi$-bounded. No graph containing a cycle is $\chi$-bounding (since graphs of arbitrarily large girth can have arbitrarily large chromatic number), so only forests can be $\chi$-bounding. Gyárfás and Sumner independently conjectured the converse. The conjecture is known for paths, brooms, subdivisions of stars, trees of radius two, and certain trees with far-apart high-degree vertices, but remains open in general.
paper_contribution: This survey reviews all known cases where the conjecture has been verified, including paths (Gyárfás), trees of radius two (Gyárfás–Szemerédi–Tuza; Kierstead–Penrice), Kierstead–Zhu trees, and new results by Scott–Seymour and Scott–Seymour–Chudnovsky on trees with far-apart high-degree vertices, while noting the conjecture remains open in full generality.
notes: Full paper text was truncated after section 3; the survey states it contains additional open problems at the end that were not present in the extracted text.
